card.intuition: A network of geographically distributed servers that work together to deliver web content (images, videos, JS) from the location closest to the user.
card.analogy: Ordering a pizza: instead of one kitchen in Italy delivery to every house in the world, the pizza company has local kitchens in every city so your pizza arrives hot and fast.
card.time_complexity: O(1) cache hit; O(N) on miss
when_to_use: Serving static assets (images, CSS, JS) to a global audience
Reducing the load on your origin (main) server
Minimizing 'Time to First Byte' (TTFB) for users thousands of miles away
gotchas: Cache invalidation (purging) is hard — if you update a file, the CDN might still serve the old version until the TTL expires
CDN costs can spiral if you don't correctly set Cache-Control headers

steps.annotation: System overview — step through to trace the flow.
User requests asset — DNS resolves to nearest CDN edge node.
Edge checks cache. HIT: serves immediately. MISS: fetches from origin.
Origin serves asset; edge caches it with TTL for future requests.

exercise.prompt: Identify the technique to ensure users always get the latest front-end assets without waiting for CDN TTLs to expire.
exercise.starter_code:
def generate_asset_url(filename):
    # Technique: add a unique hash to the filename based on content
    # e.g., main.js -> main.ae27b1.js
    file_hash = get_content_hash(filename)
    return f"{filename}.{file_hash}.js"

# This process is called Cache ____ing.
hints: You are 'busting' the cache by changing the filename
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "bust"
assert check("Bust")
print("PASS")
